source.title: Documented seed: layered architecture stack page
source.source_note: This extraction is reconstructed from the documented OpenClaw slide pattern summaries, not from the original slide screenshot.

claim: Explain architecture as a layered operating stack rather than a table or loose diagram.
visual_anchor: A vertically layered stack with clear separation between architectural layers anchors the page.

composition.structure: Main stacked architecture layers in the center or left-center with aligned detail notes on the right.
composition.alignment_logic: All layers share the same outer boundaries; labels align to one left grid and right-side notes align to the stack edge.
composition.asymmetry: The stack remains centered, while supporting annotations create controlled asymmetry.
composition.image_usage: No image is needed; geometric stack blocks and connector logic carry the page.
highlight_grammar: accent color on the critical layer or control plane
subtle gradients or glows only at layer boundaries

components.position_note: Main vertical stack with consistent boundaries
Aligned with each layer on the left
Right-side details aligned to the stack edge

why_it_works: The stack makes architecture hierarchy legible at a glance.
Consistent boundaries and spacing make a dense page feel controlled.
The page reads like a real system decomposition instead of a generic diagram.
anti_patterns: Too many layers with no visual grouping.
A matrix or table replacing the stack and losing architectural hierarchy.

do_not_copy_blindly: Exact layer naming from the source topic
adaptation_notes: Use only as many layers as the audience can parse quickly.
Preserve the stack's geometric discipline even when the content changes.
